text:
In a quiet groves corner of an old forest lived an owl. This owl was unlike anyone else. It didn't talk; it sang.

Each evening after the sun went down, feathers shimmered pink against dusk. The owl sang a haunting melody that drifted through the trees. Despite its silence, everyone knew who lived there.

One windy afternoon, a fox decided to visit.

The forest was wild around that place. Hidden streams sung secret songs. But here lived the owl—not unfriendly. Just curious.

The fox couldn't ignore the owl's song anymore.

"Why don't you sing something quieter?"

The owl didn't reply.

That night though... something changed.

The owl sang just a bit louder. And...

Suddenly voices surrounded.

"Why such lovely singing?"

They were squirrels!

"Oh, I love them!"

"You adore them!"

"Oh!..."

The forest came alive.

A couple birds took flight.

A family mouse bounced eagerly.

And just—

A big ol'o fox? Just like you
